note:
ColorStack是一个由黑人和拉丁裔工科学生组成的导师推动的社区，于5月份正式推出，但在康奈尔大学获得了成功的定位，创始人杰伦·佩蒂(Jehron Petty)成功地在三年内将该校计算机科学项目的黑人和拉丁族学生注册人数增加了两倍。现在，作为ColorStack，我们的目标是在全国更多的学校产生类似的结果。
目前，ColorStack的社区由250多所学校的600多名学生组成。
佩蒂(见下图)告诉TechCrunch：“我们能带动的主要影响来源是让他们坚持到底。”
从那时起，佩蒂就依赖于其他组织，比如dev/color，它希望让黑人和拉丁裔员工进入科技公司的职业生涯。
佩蒂说：“我们为他们解决的是下一阶段的增长--他们支持的下一批工程师来自哪里。”
对于Triplebyte来说，它的一般任务是增加获得机会的机会。TripleByte联合创始人兼首席执行官Ammon Bartram告诉TechCrunch，当公司刚成立时，一个主要目标是在种族和性别方面对多样性产生重大影响。
“一年半过去了，我惊讶和不高兴地看到，在种族和性别方面，我们的结果看起来与初创公司大同小异。我们对社会经济机会产生了很大的影响，但种族和性别这两大身份类别的比例与硅谷传统的比例相同，令人沮丧。“。
巴特拉姆说，他开始与他的顾问讨论这个问题，就在那时，Y Combinator首席执行官兼合伙人、Triplebyte董事会成员迈克尔·西贝尔鼓励巴特拉姆跳出框框思考。巴特伦说，西贝尔鼓励巴特伦像对待初创公司一样对待这个问题，尝试新事物。
Bartram说，Triplebyte考虑过赞助格雷斯·霍珀(Grace Hopper)或引入一位多元化负责人等事情，“但事实是，大多数大公司都做这些事情，但黑人和拉丁裔工程师的比例多年来几乎没有增加。”
巴特伦说：“现状被打破了，不起作用了。”“人们做了很棒的工作，但仍然没有效果。”
这就是Triplebyte如何找到进入ColorStack的方式。他们的突出之处在于他们已经取得了成功。
“这可能是一个暗淡的领域--多样性和包容性，”巴特伦说。“统计数据几乎没有什么变化。但有一件事已经被证明是有效的，那就是导师制。榜样，长得像你的人，对留在科技行业而不是离开的人有巨大的影响。但是，与他们长得很像的人组成的导师榜样，对人们选择导师、保持参与和不离开有着巨大的影响。
通过孵化，ColorStack将保持独立，同时也不必担心资金问题。此外，Triplebyte向佩蒂支付工资，并为ColorStack提供为期两年的运营资金。
“如果进展顺利，我们可能会延长，”巴特伦说。他说：“我在这里的目标是让耶伦取得成功。我们正在做的事情之间有很大的协同效应。我们是一家营利性公司，但我们很有使命感。我之所以这样做，是因为我想做一些比我过去所做的工作更有影响力的事情。成功的是ColorStack产生了影响，做了康奈尔大学曾经做过的工作，并在那里的10%的大学里复制了这一做法。这是工程界黑人和棕色人种代表人数的大幅增加。“
note_en:
ColorStack, a mentorship-driven community of Black and Latinx engineering students, officially launched in May, but got its bearings at Cornell, where founder Jehron Petty successfully worked to triple Black and Latinx enrollment in the school’s computer science program within three years. Now, as ColorStack, the goal is to produce similar outcomes at additional schools throughout the country.
 Currently, ColorStack’s community consists of more than 600 students across more than 250 schools.
 “The main source of impact we can drive is getting them to persist through the major,” Petty (pictured below) told TechCrunch.
  From there, Petty leans on other organizations, such as  dev/color, which looks to get Black and Latinx folks into careers at tech companies.
 “What we’re solving for them is this next level of growth — where the next set of engineers they support come from,” Petty said.
 For Triplebyte, its general mission operative is to increase access to opportunities. When the company first launched, a major goal was to have a significant impact on diversity in terms of race and gender, TripleByte co-founder and CEO Ammon Bartram told TechCrunch.
 “One-and-a-half years in, I was surprised and unhappy to see that, in terms of race and gender, our results looked pretty much identical to startups. We had a big impact on socioeconomic opportunity, but the big identity categories of race and gender were in the same depressing ratios of Silicon Valley’s traditional ratio.”
 Bartram said he began talking to his advisors about the issue and that’s when Y Combinator CEO and partner Michael Seibel, who is a board member at Triplebyte, encouraged Bartram to think outside of the box. Seibel encouraged Bartram to treat the problem like a startup and to try new things, Bartram said.
 Triplebyte considered things like sponsoring Grace Hopper, or bringing in a head of diversity, “but the truth of the matter is, the majority of larger companies do these things but the percentage of Black and Latinx engineers has barely increased over the years,” Bartram said.
 “The status quo is broken and not working,” Bartram said. “People have done great work but it’s still not working.”
 This is how Triplebyte found its way to ColorStack. What stood out about them was the success they were already having.
 “It can be a bleak field — diversity and inclusion,” Bartram said. “There’s been so little change in the stats. But one thing that has been shown to work is mentorship. Role models, people who look like you, have a huge impact on people staying in tech and not leaving. But the mentorship role model, with people who look like them, has a huge impact on people choosing a mentor, staying involved and not leaving.”
 Through the incubation, ColorStack will remain independent while also not having to worry about funding. Additionally, Triplebyte pays Petty a salary and provided ColorStack with operating funds for two years.
 “If it goes well, we might extend it,” Bartram said. “My goal here is for Jehron to succeed. There’s lots of synergy between what we’re doing. We’re a for-profit company but we’re pretty mission driven. I’m doing this because I want to do something more impactful than the work I did in the past. Success would be ColorStack having an impact and doing the work that worked at Cornell and duplicating that at even 10% of the colleges out there. That’s a huge increase in representation of Black and brown folks in engineering.”
title: Triplebyte孵化ColorStack以增加CS程序中的黑色和Latinx表示
title_en: Triplebyte incubates ColorStack to increase Black and Latinx representation in CS programs

note:
“有一种‘到处都是水，但没有一滴水可喝’的现象，”韦克斯曼说。“有3000多种狗粮，但我认为没有一种是不假思索、令人信服的答案。”
Sunday在2月份“软”推出了它的第一款产品，现在大约有1000名付费客户。它今天将在更广泛的范围内推出，并宣布已经从红海风险投资公司、Box Group、Great Oaks Ventures、Matt Salzberg、Zach Klein和其他公司筹集了227万美元的资金。
韦克斯曼过去的初创公司包括约会应用Grouper，而他的妻子兼联合创始人托里·韦克斯曼是一名兽医(并担任初创公司的首席兽医官)。他告诉我，他们俩几年前开始对宠物食品感兴趣，当时他们的一只狗开始胃部有问题，他们“走进了这个兔子洞，试图找到最好的狗食。”
韦克斯曼说，市场可以分为两大类。还有Kibble，它相对便宜，负担得起，但不是那么健康。然后是冷藏食品，包括直接面向消费者的选择，如农夫狗，它们更健康，但也更贵，需要更多的准备。
“这些都太不方便了，”韦克斯曼辩称。“你不会发现太多人比我们更爱他们的狗，我们会为我们的狗做任何事情--除了每天为它们准备一个小时的食物。”
因此，他将“周日”定位为“介于谷仓和冷藏之间的第三类新狗粮”。它应该是人类级别的狗粮，90%是新鲜的肉、器官和骨头，通过独特的风干过程制造出来。
韦克斯曼说，对于依赖Kibble的狗主人来说，这家初创公司提供了“更高质量的产品，味道要好得多，而且不会影响你习惯的便利性”，而对于目前为冷藏选择付费的主人，他承诺“在质量和口味上没有任何妥协，全面提高了便利性，令人难以置信。”
几位早期顾客在评论中将这种食物比作牛肉干。韦克斯曼补充说，在品尝测试中，狗更喜欢周日而不是优质吉卜狗，比例为40比0。
这些食物既可以一次性购买，也可以订阅购买。目前，一盒40盎司的盒子售价为75美元，而同样的盒子通过订阅的价格为59美元。
韦克斯曼认为，要达到这一点并不容易，因为有了制造狗粮的新工艺，“没有为此建立供应链。”最终，他说，周日选择了“美国农业部监控的美国肉干厨房来创造这种新的外形因素。”
“我们花的时间比我们预想的要长得多，”他承认。“然而，短期头痛是我们真正感到兴奋的长期特征。归根结底，它应该是一条相当深的护城河，以防止潜在竞争对手提供同样高质量和差异化的产品。
note_en:
“There’s a sort of ‘Water everywhere, but not a drop to drink’ phenomenon,” Waxman said. “There are over 3,000 dog foods, and yet I think there isn’t really one that is the no-brainer, compelling answer.”
 Sundays “soft launched” its first product in February and now has around 1,000 paying customers. It’s launching more broadly today and is also announcing that it has raised $2.27 million in funding from  Red Sea Ventures,   Box Group, Great Oaks Ventures, Matt Salzberg, Zach Klein and others.
  Waxman’s past startups include  dating app Grouper, while his wife/co-founder Tory Waxman is a veterinarian (and serves as the startup’s chief veterinary officer). He told me that the two of them became interested in pet food a couple years ago when one of their dogs started to have stomach issues, and they “went down this rabbit hole of trying to find the best dog food.”
 The market can be divided two broad categories, Waxman said. There’s kibble, which is relatively cheap and affordable but not as healthy. Then there’s refrigerated food, including direct-to-consumer options  like The Farmer’s Dog, which are healthier but also pricier and require more preparation.
 “Those are so unbelievably inconvenient,” Waxman argued. “You’re not going to find too many people crazier about their dogs than we are, and we would do literally anything for our dogs — except prepare their food for an hour a day.”
  So he’s pitching Sundays as a “new, third category of dog food between kibble and refrigerated.” It’s supposed to be human-grade dog food that’s 90% fresh meat, organs and bones, created through a unique air drying process.
 For dog owners who rely on kibble, Waxman said the startup offers “a much higher-quality product that tastes much better and doesn’t compromise on the convenience that you’re used to,” while for owners who currently pay for refrigerated options, he promised “an all-around unbelievable increase in convenience, without any compromise in quality and taste.”
 Several early customers compared the food to beef jerky in their  reviews. Waxman added that in taste tests, dogs preferred Sundays to premium kibble 40-to-0.
 The food is available for both one-time and subscription purchase. A single 40-ounce box currently costs $75, while the same box costs $59 via subscription.
 Waxman suggested that it hasn’t been easy getting to this point — with a new process for creating dog food, “there were no supply chains set up for this.” Ultimately, he said Sundays selected a “USDA-monitored jerky kitchen in the U.S. to create this new form factor.”
 “It took us much longer than we expected,” he admitted. “However, the short-term headache is a long-term feature that we’re really excited about. Ultimately, it should serve as a pretty deep moat to prevent would-be competitors from offering similarly high quality and differentiated products.”
title: 狗粮初创公司Sunday推出风干吉布替代品
title_en: Dog food startup Sundays launches its air-dried kibble alternative

Khosrowshahi说：“很难相信我们能够迅速将我们的模式转变为全职工作。”他指出，这将迫使公司暂时关闭。Khosrowshahi的言论与优步周二在法院裁决后提交的动议中的措辞一致。
周一，加州高等法院法官伊桑·舒尔曼(Ethan Schulman)发布了一项初步禁令，迫使优步和Lyft将司机重新归类为员工。这项命令将在10天后生效。法官承认，该命令将以“重大方式”改变优步和Lyft商业行为的性质，执行禁令将“代价高昂”。然而，这些困难并不足以动摇法院将司机归类为员工的决定，这一决定将迫使优步和Lyft提供失业保险和其他福利。
加州司法部长泽维尔·贝塞拉(Xavier Becera)与来自洛杉矶、圣地亚哥和旧金山的城市检察官一起对优步和Lyft提起诉讼，以迫使这两家公司遵守AB5。
优步的律师在周二提交的动议中要求，在上诉法院就是否应该维持这一裁决做出决定期间，应该暂缓发布禁制令。律师们辩称，“如果禁令生效，优步几乎肯定会被迫关闭加州的打车平台，这将对优步和所有依赖其打车应用为他们及其家人创造收入的人造成不可挽回的伤害-特别是在疫情肆虐的情况下。”
note_en:
“It’s hard to believe we’ll be able to switch our model to full-time employment quickly,” Khosrowshahi said, noting it would force the company to temporarily shut down. Khosrowshahi’s comments are consistent to language in a motion filed Tuesday by Uber following the court’s ruling.
   On Monday, California Superior Court Judge Ethan Schulman granted a preliminary injunction forcing Uber and Lyft to  reclassify its drivers as employees. This order is set to go into effect in 10 days. The judge acknowledged that the order would change the nature of Uber and Lyft’s business practices in “significant ways” and implementing the injunction would be “costly.” However, those hardships weren’t enough to sway the court from classifying drivers as employees, a decision that would force Uber and Lyft to provide unemployment insurance and other benefits.
 California Attorney General Xavier Becerra, along with city attorneys from Los Angeles, San Diego and San Francisco, brought the lawsuit against Uber and Lyft to force the companies to comply with AB 5.
 Uber’s attorneys requested in its motion filed Tuesday that an injunction should be stayed while the Court of Appeals makes its decision over whether the ruling should stand. The attorneys argued that “Uber will almost certainly be forced to shut off the Rides platform in California if the injunction goes into effect, which would irreparably harm Uber and all who rely on its Rides app to generate income for them and their families — particularly in the midst of a pandemic.”
title: 优步首席执行官表示，由于工人重新分类的裁决，服务在加州面临暂时关闭
title_en: Uber CEO says service faces temporary shutdown in California over worker reclassification ruling

色调播放HDMI同步盒的核心是一个HDMI切换器，提供四个HDMI输入和一个HDMI输出。来自输入设备(如Apple TV、Roku、Xbox、PS4等)的信号。进入盒子，然后传到电视上，切换会根据最近激活的设备自动发生(您也可以使用应用程序和语音控制手动更改它们)。
Sync Box支持一系列现代显示和音频质量标准。它支持4K 60 Hz分辨率、HDR10+和杜比视觉标准，以及杜比Atmos环绕立体声。它还支持符合HDCP 2.2标准的HDMI 2.0b，以实现版权保护。
你不仅需要色调彩灯，还需要色桥(第二代圆形正方形版本)，以确保色调同步盒不仅仅是一个特别昂贵的HDMI集线器，而且它也能很好地完成这项工作。如果您确实有色调产品，如可以轻松安装在电视支架顶部或电视背面的Hue Play灯条，或者Hue Signe彩色落地灯或台灯，那么您可以使用Sync配套应用程序来确保您的灯光反映屏幕上正在发生的事情-播放来自任何来源的任何视频。
你为什么想要这个？嗯，主要是因为它看起来真的，真的很酷。色调同步已经作为软件功能提供，当您与监控工具结合使用时，可以在Mac和PC上回放视频。但这有很多限制，包括无法在浏览器中使用官方Netflix应用程序和Netflix。Sync Box消除了任何潜在的障碍，还意味着您可以使用常规的流媒体和游戏源，而不必运行媒体中心PC。
盒子本身相对较大，但这似乎主要是为了容纳多个HDMI端口。它非常短，尽管它的表面积大约是Apple TV的两倍，所以它应该很容易集成到现有的家庭影院设置中，无论这需要什么。
设置色调播放HDMI同步盒非常简单，只需安装应用程序，并在提示时按下色桥上的同步按钮即可。如前所述，你最多可以插入四个信号源，当你使用输入设备时，框将自动在它们之间切换，或者你也可以使用应用程序手动更改输入(并重命名它们)。这款应用程序还允许你调整光线的强度、亮度和响应度，使其变得更微妙或更极端，这取决于你的偏好和你的活动。例如，“游戏”设置将其设置为最大强度和响应度，以获得更适合快节奏互动内容的动态效果。
我发现，灯光在模仿场景的颜色和亮度方面表现得非常好，特别是如果你花时间在官方Main Hue应用程序中准确地设置你的色调灯的位置，用于专门的“娱乐区”。这是一种效果，当在其最微妙的设置下使用时，基本上可以淡出，但仍然可以真正增强观看体验，使其感觉更具身临其境的感觉。在最大限度的设置下，它会更引人注目，但在长时间使用后，它仍然会以一种好的方式基本上消失在背景中。
特别是自从固件更新后，色调播放同步盒已经证明是对我的家庭影院设置的一个奇妙的补充，为每一个看电视的体验提供了额外的天赋。它显然在黑暗的房间里更有效，但它似乎真的特别能补充高质量的OLED屏幕，这些屏幕能产生鲜艳的色彩和真正的深黑色。
Hue play HDMI Sync Box售价229.99美元，有点奢侈，但它无疑增加了电影、流媒体和游戏的整体家庭电视观看体验。四个HDMI输入意味着您还可以使用它为您的电视添加更多端口(如果您需要的话)，而最近的更新意味着您在这样做的同时不会牺牲任何视频质量。
note_en:
The Hue Play HDMI Sync Box is at its core an HDMI switcher, offering four HDMI inputs and a single HDMI output. Signals from your input devices (e.g., Apple TV,  Roku,   Xbox, PS4, etc.) go into the box and are passed through to the TV, with switching happening automatically depending on which device was most recently active (you can also change them manually with the app and with voice controls).
 The Sync Box supports a range of modern quality standards for display and audio. It supports 4K 60Hz resolution, HDR10+ and Dolby Vision standards, as well as Dolby Atmos surround sound. It also supports HDMI 2.0b with HDCP 2.2 compliance for copyright protection.
  You will need not only Hue colored lights, but also a Hue Bridge (the second-generation, rounded-square version) to ensure that the Hue Sync Box is more than just a particularly expensive HDMI hub, but it does that job very well, too. If you do have Hue products, like the Hue Play light bars that can easily mount on top of your TV stand or to the back of your TV itself, or the Hue Signe multicolored floor or table lamps, then you can use the Sync companion app to ensure your lights reflect what’s going on on screen — for any video that plays through the box from any source.
   Why would you want this? Well, mostly because it looks really, really cool. Hue Sync has already been available as a software feature for you to use with video played back on Macs and PCs when used in combination with a monitoring tool. But that has a lot of limitations, including not being able to work with official  Netflix   apps and Netflix in the browser. The Sync Box eliminates any potential roadblocks and also means you can use regular streaming and gaming sources without having to run a media center PC.
 The box itself is relatively large, but that seems like it’s mostly to accommodate the multiple HDMI ports. It’s very short, despite being about twice the surface area of an Apple TV, so it should be very easy to integrate into your existing home theatre setup, whatever that entails.
 Setting up the Hue Play HDMI Sync Box is very easy and requires only installing the app and pressing the sync button on your Hue Bridge when instructed to do so. As mentioned, you can plug in up to four sources and the box will switch between them automatically when you use an input device or you can also manually change the input (and rename them) using the app. The app also allows you to tweak the intensity, brightness and responsiveness of the light, making it more subtle or more extreme, depending on your preferences and your activity. A “Game” setting, for instance, sets it to maximum intensity and responsiveness for a more dynamic effect befitting fast-paced interactive content.
  I found that the lighting was extremely good at mimicking the colors and brightness of a scene, especially if you take the time to accurately set up the position of your Hue lights for a dedicated “entertainment area” in the official main Hue app. It’s an effect that, when used in its most subtle settings, can basically fade away but still provide genuine enhancement for the watching experience, making it feel more immersive. At its maxed out settings, it’s much more noticeable, but still something that basically fades away into the background over an extended period of use, in a good way.
 Especially since the firmware update, the Hue Play Sync Box has proven a fantastic addition to my home theater setup, providing an extra bit of flair to every TV watching experience. It’s obviously more effective in dark rooms, but it really seems to especially complement high-quality OLED screens that produce vibrant colors and true, deep blacks.
  The Hue Play HDMI Sync Box is a bit of an extravagance at $229.99, but it definitely adds to the overall home TV-watching experience, for movies, streaming and for gaming. The four HDMI inputs mean you can also use it to add more ports to your TV, if that’s something you need, and the recent updates mean you’re not going to sacrifice any video quality while doing so.
title: 飞利浦色调游戏HDMI Sync Box使任何家庭影院都更具戏剧性
title_en: The Philips Hue Play HDMI Sync Box makes any home theater a bit more theatrical

note:
十年前，创始人们会去沙山路两旁古板的投资者办公室朝圣。现在，随着冠状病毒肆虐全球，风险资本作为一种资产类别不断增长，第一个“是”可能来自建立在概念之上的投资者匹配工具，或者整个基金可以通过Zoom Call聚集在一起。在这个时代，Twitter DM更适合交易流程，而不是在会议上走动。
随着创业之地变得越来越非正式，新一代早期创业者正在寻找方法，让轻松的新世界对他们有利。这是一种发生的方式吗？
在Couplet Coffee的创始人格芬·斯科尔尼克(Gefen Skolnick)推出她的公司和面向代表不足、资源不足的科技群体的松弛频道之前，她以一种独特的方式建立了信誉。
斯科尔尼克是2020年震动硅谷的眼口眼(👁👄👁)活动的一部分。这一隐晦的努力是对FOMO和炒作文化如何主导风险投资对话的一种声明。这项活动的参与者更改了他们的Twitter名字，隐秘地发推文，并为一种甚至不存在的产品赢得了2万多名电子邮件订户。
斯科尔尼克说，Eye Mate Eye给了她一个更大的平台，她正在通过加快发布新内容和推出产品的步伐来利用这个平台。她说，这一噱头让她得到了知名人士的“认可”，投资者一直在炸毁她的收件箱。
她说：“我的筹款经历就是天使投资者让我知道他们在投资。”
她说，模因文化是“年轻一代以更易于理解和分享的形式展示他们的洞察力、幽默感和评论的最佳方式”。
note_en:
A decade ago, founders would make pilgrimages to the stodgy investor offices that line Sand Hill Road. Now, as the coronavirus ravages the world and venture capital grows as an asset class, a first “yes” can come from  an investor-matching tool built on Notion, or an entire fund  can come together over a Zoom call. In this era, Twitter DMs are better for deal flow than walking around a conference.
 As startup-land becomes more informal, a new generation of early-stage founders are searching for ways to make the relaxed new world work in their favor. One way this is happening?
  Before  Gefen Skolnick, founder of  Couplet Coffee, launched her company and Slack channel for underrepresented and underresourced groups in tech, she established credibility in a unique way.
 Skolnick was part of the  Eye Mouth Eye ( 👁👄👁) campaign that rocked Silicon Valley in June 2020. The cryptic effort was a statement on how FOMO and hype culture dominate venture capital conversations. Participants in the  campaign changed their Twitter names, tweeted cryptically and earned more than 20,000 email subscribers for a product that did not even exist.
 Skolnick says Eye Mouth Eye gave her a larger platform, which she’s leveraging by stepping up the pace of posting new content and launching products. She said the stunt gave her “sign-offs” from high-profile individuals, and investors have been blowing up her inbox.
 “My fundraising experience has just been angel investors DMing me to tell me they’re investing,” she said.
 Meme culture, she says, is the “best way the younger generation can showcase their insights, humor and commentary in a more digestible and shareable format.”
title: 👁👄👁是否说明了表情包文化在筹款中的力量？
title_en: Does 👁👄👁 illustrate the power of meme culture in fundraising?

note:
李天辉是数据孵化器的创始人，这是一个为期8周的奖学金，旨在帮助博士和博士后从学术界过渡到行业。此前，他在Foursquare负责货币化数据科学，曾在谷歌(Google)、安德森·霍洛维茨(Andreessen Horowitz)、摩根大通(J.P.Morgan)和D.E.Shaw工作过。
从历史上看，数据科学招聘实践是从软件工程演变而来的。软件工程面试的一个标志是可怕的脑筋急转弯，比如“一架波音747能装多少个高尔夫球？”或者“在白板上实现快速排序算法。”应聘者将为此学习几周或几个月，招聘网站Glassdoor有一个完整的部分专门介绍他们。在数据科学中，传统的编码脑筋急转弯也得到了统计学的补充--“两个掷骰子的和被三整除的概率是多少？”多年来，公司开始意识到这些脑筋急转弯并不是特别有效，并开始减少它们的使用。
取而代之的是，公司正专注于基于项目的数据评估。这些要求数据科学考生分析公司提供的真实数据。基于项目的评估通常更开放，更鼓励探索，而不是只有一个正确的答案。受访者通常会提交代码，并写下他们的结果。无论是在形式上还是在实质上，这些都有许多优势。
首先，数据评估的环境要现实得多。脑筋急转弯不必要地让应试者陷入困境，或者迫使他们在白板上笨拙地编码。因为脑筋急转弯的答案很容易在谷歌上找到，所以互联网资源是禁区。在工作中，你不太可能被要求在白板上编程，或者在有人盯着你看的情况下进行心算。令人费解的是，你会在工作时间被拒绝上网。数据评估还允许申请者使用他们喜欢的IDE或编码环境，以更现实的速度完成评估。
工程经理肖恩·格里什(Sean Gerrish)说，“带回家的挑战让你有机会更真实地模拟应聘者在工作中的表现，而不是困惑的面试问题。”格里什著有“智能机器是如何思考的”(How Smart Machines Think)一书。
第二，数据评估的实质也更现实。从设计上讲，脑筋急转弯要么很棘手，要么就是测试知名算法的知识。在现实生活中，人们永远不会用手写这些算法(你会使用互联网上免费提供的几十种解决方案中的一种)，工作中遇到的问题也很少以同样的方式棘手。通过向应聘者提供他们可能使用的真实数据，并根据公司实际分享结果的方式组织交付成果，数据项目与实际工作技能更紧密地结合在一起。
业内资深人士、“数据团队”(Data Teams)一书的作者杰西·安德森(Jesse Anderson)是数据评估的铁杆粉丝：“这是一个互惠互利的设置。受访者被给予一个模仿现实世界的战斗机会。经理们更接近于在工作中考察应聘者的工作和能力。基于项目的评估还有一个额外的好处，就是评估书面沟通能力，这在新冠肺炎的在家工作中是一项越来越重要的技能。
最后，书面的技术项目工作可以通过淡化招聘过程中传统但有偏见的方面来帮助避免偏见。带有西班牙裔和非裔美国人名字的简历收到的回调比带有白人名字的简历少。对此，少数族裔应聘者故意“美化”简历以弥补损失。面对面的面试通常依赖于类似的问题直觉。通过强调与工作表现密切相关的评估，面试官可以将精力集中在实际资质上，而不是依赖潜在的有偏见的“本能”。希望接受#BLM和#MeToo而不是标签的公司可能会考虑如何调整他们的招聘流程，以实现更大的平等。
数据评估的确切形式各不相同。在数据孵化器，我们发现超过60%的公司提供带回家的数据评估。这些最好地模拟了实际的工作环境，允许应聘者在家工作几天(通常情况下)。另有大约20%的人需要面试数据项目，应聘者将分析数据作为面试过程的一部分。虽然考生面临着来自这些方面的更大时间压力，但他们也没有感受到不断进行评估的压力。经验丰富的数据科学家、“数据科学手册”(The Data Science Handbook)的作者菲尔德·卡迪(Field Cady)解释说：“带回家的挑战需要很多时间。”“对于考生来说，这是一件很大的家务，(例如)对那些承担不起在挑战上花费太多晚上时间的家庭承诺的人来说，这可能是不公平的。”
为了减少定制数据项目的数量，聪明的应聘者正在先发制人地构建自己的投资组合项目，以展示他们的技能，公司越来越多地接受这些项目来代替定制工作。
依赖老式脑筋急转弯的公司正在消失。在顽固的20%的雇主中，仍然坚持脑筋急转弯的雇主中，大多数是规模更大、更成熟的企业，它们通常适应变化的速度更慢。他们需要认识到，过时的招聘流程不仅看起来古怪，而且还在积极地将应聘者赶走。在最近的一次虚拟会议上，我的一位小组同事是一名数据科学的新员工，他解释说，由于公司糟糕的筛选流程，他拒绝了机会。
如果招聘流程如此过时，团队还能有多强呢？完成数据孵化器数据科学奖学金的博士们也普遍认同这种观点。未能接受新现实的公司正在输掉争夺顶尖人才的战斗。
note_en:
Tianhui Michael Li is founder of  The Data Incubator, an eight-week fellowship to help PhDs and postdocs transition from academia into industry. Previously, he headed monetization data science at Foursquare and has worked at Google, Andreessen Horowitz, J.P. Morgan and D.E. Shaw.
  Historically, data science hiring practices evolved from software engineering. A hallmark of software engineering interviewing is the dreaded brain teaser, puzzles like “How many golf balls would fit inside a Boeing 747?” or “Implement the quick-sort algorithm on the whiteboard.” Candidates will study for weeks or months for these and the hiring website  Glassdoor   has an entire section  devoted to them. In data science, the traditional coding brain teaser has been supplemented with statistics ones as well — “What is the probability that the sum of two dice rolls is divisible by three?” Over the years, companies are starting to realize that these brain teasers are not terribly effective and have started cutting down their usage.
 In their place, firms are focusing on project-based data assessments. These ask data science candidates to analyze real-world data provided by the company. Rather than having a single correct answer, project-based assessments are often more open-ended, encouraging exploration. Interviewees typically submit code and a write-up of their results. These have a number of advantages, both in terms of form and substance.
  First, the environment for data assessments is far more realistic. Brain teasers unnecessarily put candidates on the spot or compel them to awkwardly code on a whiteboard. Because answers to brain teasers are readily Google-able, internet resources are off-limits. On the job, it is unlikely that you’ll be asked to code on a whiteboard or perform mental math with someone peering over your shoulder. It is incomprehensible that you’ll be denied internet access during work hours. Data assessments also allow the applicants to complete the assessment at a more realistic pace, using their favorite  IDE or coding environment.
 “Take-home challenges give you a chance to simulate how the candidate will perform on the job more realistically than with puzzle interview questions,” said  Sean Gerrish, an engineering manager and author of “ How Smart Machines Think.”
 Second, the substance of data assessments is also more realistic. By design, brainteasers are tricky or test knowledge of well-known algorithms. In real life, one would never write these algorithms by hand (you would use one of the dozens of solutions freely available on the internet) and the problems encountered on the job are rarely tricky in the same way. By giving candidates real data they might work with and structuring the deliverable in line with how results are actually shared at the company, data projects are more closely aligned with actual job skills.
 Jesse Anderson, an industry veteran and author of “ Data Teams,” is a big fan of data assessments: “It’s a mutually beneficial setup. Interviewees are given a fighting chance that mimics the real-world. Managers get closer to an on-the-job look at a candidate’s work and abilities.” Project-based assessments have the added benefit of assessing written communication strength, an increasingly important skill in the work-from-home world of COVID-19.
 Finally, written technical project work can help avoid bias by de-emphasizing traditional but prejudicially fraught aspects of the hiring process. Resumes with  Hispanic and African American names receive fewer callbacks than the same resume with white names. In response,  minority candidates deliberately “whiten” their resumes to compensate. In-person interviews often rely on similarly problematic  gut feel. By emphasizing an assessment closely tied to job performance, interviewers can focus their energies on actual qualifications, rather than relying on potentially biased “instincts.” Companies looking to embrace #BLM and #MeToo beyond hashtagging may consider how tweaking their hiring processes can lead to greater equality.
 The exact form of data assessments vary. At The Data Incubator, we found that over 60% of firms provide take-home data assessments. These best simulate the actual work environment, allowing the candidate to work from home (typically) over the course of a few days. Another roughly 20% require interview data projects, where candidates analyze data as a part of the interview process. While candidates face more time pressure from these, they also do not feel the pressure to ceaselessly work on the assessment. “Take-home challenges take a lot of time,” explains  Field Cady, an experienced data scientist and author of “ The Data Science Handbook.” “This is a big chore for candidates and can be unfair (for example) to people with family commitments who can’t afford to spend many evening hours on the challenge.”
 To reduce the number of custom data projects, smart candidates are preemptively building their own portfolio projects to showcase their skills and companies are increasingly accepting these in lieu of custom work.
 Companies relying on old-fashioned brainteasers are a vanishing breed. Of the recalcitrant 20% of employers still sticking with brainteasers, most are the larger, more established enterprises that are usually slower to adapt to change. They need to realize that the antiquated hiring process doesn’t just look quaint, it’s actively driving candidates away. At a recent virtual conference, one of my fellow panelists was a data science new hire who explained that he had turned down opportunities based on the firm’s poor screening process.
 How strong can the team be if the hiring process is so outmoded? This sentiment is also widely shared by the Ph.D.s completing The Data Incubator’s data science fellowship. Companies that fail to embrace the new reality are losing the battle for top talent.
title: 2020年雇佣数据科学家有什么不同？
title_en: What’s different about hiring data scientists in 2020?

note:
三星生产的智能手表不错。事实往往会在关于一个传统上由单一玩家主导的类别的讨论中迷失。在全球范围内，情况最近发生了一些变化。苹果的市场份额有所下滑，华为和三星也在那里收拾残局。
在美国，华为扮演的角色不那么重要，原因应该是显而易见的，令人痛苦。谷歌的Wear OS-至少目前-是不可能的。收购目标Fitbit凭借其坚固的智能手表做出了重大贡献，尽管它并不是一个强大的公司。Garmin也是如此，它做得很好，但利基市场相对较小。对于那些希望与Android兼容的人--或者通常只是苹果的替代品--三星仍然是你最好的选择。
长期以来，该公司对待可穿戴设备的态度与管理其智能手表的理念相似：选择多，功能多，占地面积大，华而不实。然而，在我看来，Galaxy Watch 3最重要的功能是旋转表圈的恢复。该公司最近放弃了它，转而推出活跃的产品线，试图说服我们，触觉近似也是一样好的。不是这样的。
长期以来，通过旋转显示屏边框在屏幕之间切换的能力一直是Gear系列最显著的特点-也是迄今为止最好的智能手表输入。苹果手表的桂冠还差得很远。对于三星来说，放弃它是一个奇怪的选择，即使是对于一个分裂出来的手表系列来说也是如此。值得庆幸的是，它的背面由与表身其余部分相同的不锈钢外壳制成，并带有穿孔凸缘，以获得更好的抓地力。在屏幕之间平稳旋转的能力令人满意。
这一次，挡板稍微薄了一点，与外壳大小的整体缩小相匹配。总体而言，3比最初的Galaxy Watch薄了14%，小了8%，轻了15%。与其他智能手表相比，45 mm的机型仍然很大，但这在一定程度上解决了我长期以来对该系列的主要抱怨之一。41毫米版本的可用性也是如此。过去的Galaxy手表给人的感觉是不必要的笨重-这是一个打算整天戴在身上的设备的问题。
这款手表对我个人的品味来说还是有点大，但在主动线之外，这绝对是一段时间以来最舒适的三星手表。就智能手表而言，金属外壳也有相当永恒的设计，保持了几代人以来一直是该系列标准的运动外观。我更喜欢简约一点的东西。对我来说，S2过去和现在都是该系列设计语言的巅峰，但我认识到很多人更喜欢…一点的设计语言。复杂。
45 mm的屏幕尺寸为1.4英寸，41 mm的屏幕尺寸为1.2英寸，比Apple Watch的1.65/1.5英寸要小。不过，很明显，圆形设计也提供了不同的外形因素。屏幕漂亮、清晰，在白天读起来也很好，这在一定程度上要归功于环境光传感器。这款机型配有漂亮的皮表带，由于其标准化的合身，基本上可以与不同的第三方表带进行交换。
Tizen似乎一直是一个奇怪的选择，但三星在很大程度上让这款操作系统成为了自己的选择，因为谷歌一直在努力让自己的可穿戴操作系统得到更广泛的采用。这款手表的应用选择继续落后于苹果，包括一些大名鼎鼎的开发者。这里有一些重要的合作伙伴-最引人注目的是Spotify。随着苹果在手表和流媒体领域都大展身手，这一合作对双方都很有意义。其他关键功能之一是能够将播放列表直接下载到设备上，这样如果你愿意的话，你可以把手机留在家里锻炼。
而Galaxy商店在应用程序方面的不足，它远远弥补了Watch Faces的不足，目前有超过8万个应用程序可用。还有大约40种不同的模块化并发症。您还可以通过拍摄真实世界图案的图像来创建自定义面。
电池续航时间还不错，只要你关掉一直开着的显示屏。通过这样做，我能够在整整两天的生活中少花几个小时。这并不例外，但它应该会帮助你利用一周中的几天的睡眠跟踪，假设你手腕上戴着一块相当大的手表就能舒服地入睡。诚然，这需要一些时间来适应。在启用Always-On的情况下，您可以预期获得总数的一半左右。
这款手表能很好地自动检测精选的运动。跑步分析更深入一些，增加了Active 2引入的一项功能。按摩完成后，它会细分跑步机制的细节(尽可能作为手腕磨损的监视器)，以帮助减少与跑步相关的伤害。作为一名目前的前跑步者，我可以证明这样一个事实：糟糕的状态真的是伤害你自己的好方法。
随着苹果终于在其手表上推出睡眠跟踪系列，三星也在完善自己的体验。这款手表分解了浅睡眠、深睡眠和快速眼动睡眠，为当晚提供了一个分数。我发现自己在40多岁的时候就拿到了分数--不算太高，因为在我这个年龄段的人显然得分在70分左右。三星还提供了专注力和压力管理等功能来控制这种情况。就我个人而言，我认为要想睡得更好，我的这一端将采取“不是一直在想COVID”的功能。
在美国，有两个关键的健康补充功能还没有准备好：EKG读取器和血压检测仪都必须等待所有标准的监管批准，所以我会相应地推迟判断。
三星当然不会试图在这里的价格上具有竞争力。41 mm和45 mm版本的售价分别为400美元和430美元，这是一个溢价。很明显，该公司并不将华为甚至Fitbit这样的公司视为其主要竞争对手。就像其旗舰智能手机一样，三星牢牢盯上了苹果，而且它的价格也与苹果不相上下。苹果无疑仍然是iOS用户的最佳选择，但当谈到Android时，没有多少人能与三星的高端产品竞争。
note_en:
Samsung makes good smartwatches. The fact tends to get lost in discussions about a category that’s traditionally been so dominated by a single player. Things have shifted a bit of late, globally. Apple’s  market share has slipped somewhat and Huawei and Samsung have been there to pick up some of the pieces.
 Here in the States, Huawei is less of a player for reasons that should be painfully obvious. Google’s Wear OS is — for now at least — a non-starter. Acquisition target Fitbit has made a dent with its solid smartwatches, though it’s not quite a juggernaut. Same goes for Garmin, which does well, but commands a relatively small niche. For those looking for Android compatibility — or just an Apple alternative, generally — Samsung continues to be your best shot.
 The company has long approached its wearables with a similar philosophy to the one that governs its smartwatches: lots of options, plenty of features and a big, flashy footprint. For my money, however, the top-line feature on the Galaxy Watch 3 is the return of the rotating bezel. The company recently abandoned it for its Active line, attempting to convince us that a haptic approximation was just as good. It wasn’t.
   The ability to toggle between screens by spinning the border of the display has long been the Gear line’s most distinguishing characteristic — and the best smartwatch input by far. The Apple Watch crown isn’t even close. It was an odd choice for Samsung to drop it, even for a splinter line of watches. It’s back, thankfully, made from the same stainless steel casing as the rest of the watch body and sporting a perforated ridge for a better grip. There’s something satisfying in the ability to smoothly spin between screens.
 The bezel is a bit thinner this time out, matching an overall reduction in case size. All told, the 3 is 14% thinner, 8% smaller and 15% lighter than the original Galaxy Watch. The 45mm model is still large, compared to other smartwatches, but this goes a ways toward addressing what’s long been one of my chief complaints with the line. Ditto for the availability of a 41mm version. Past Galaxy Watches have felt needlessly bulky — an issue with a device intended to wear on one’s person all day and night.
  The watch is still a bit big for my personal tastes, but outside of the Active line, this is definitely the most comfortable Samsung watch in some time. The metallic casing also has a fairly timeless design, as far as smartwatches go, maintaining a sporty look that’s been a standard of the line for a few generations now. I tend to prefer something a bit more minimalistic. For me, the S2 was and continues to be the pinnacle of design language for the line, but I recognize that plenty of people prefer something a bit more…complex.
 The screen measures 1.4 inches on the 45mm and 1.2 inches on the 41mm — a touch smaller than the 1.65/1.5 inches found on the Apple Watch. Though, obviously the round design also offers up a different form factor. The screen is nice and clear and reads pretty well in daylight, thanks in part to the ambient light sensor. The model ships with a nice leather band, and thanks to its standardized fit, can be swapped out with an essentially infinite list of different third-party bands.
  Tizen   has always seemed like an odd choice, but Samsung’s very much made the operating system its own here, as Google has struggled for wider adoption with its own wearable OS. The watch’s app selection continues to lag Apple, including some bigger-name developers. There are some important partners here — most notably Spotify. With Apple making major plays on both the watch and streaming front, the partnership makes a lot of sense for both parties. Among other key features is the ability to download playlists directly to the device, so you can leave the phone at home for a workout, if you’re so inclined.
 And what the Galaxy Store lacks in apps, it more than makes up for in watch faces, with more than 80,000 currently available. There are also some 40 different modular complications. You can also create custom faces by taking an image of real world patterns.
 Battery life is decent, as long as you turn off the always-on display. By doing so, I was able to get a couple of hours short of two full days of life. That’s not exceptional, but it should help you take advantage of the sleep tracking a few days a week, assume you’re comfortable falling a sleep with sizable watch on your wrist. That admittedly takes some getting used to. With alway-on enabled, you can expect to get about half that total.
  The watch does a good job autodetecting select workouts. Running analysis goes a bit deeper, adding to a feature introduced with the Active 2. After a rub is complete, it breaks down the specifics of your run mechanics (as best it can as a wrist-worn monitor) in an attempt to help reduce running-related injury. As a current former runner, I can attest to the fact that poor form is a real good way to injure yourself.
 As Apple is finally getting series about sleep tracking on its watch, Samsung is smoothing out its own experience. The watch breaks down light, deep and REM sleep, offering up a score for the night. I found myself getting scores in the 40s — not great, given that people in my age range apparently score around a 70. Samsung also offers up features like mindfulness and stress management to get that under control. Personally, I think getting better sleep on   my end is going to take “not constantly thinking about COVID” feature.
  Two key health additions aren’t ready out of the box here in the states: both the EKG reader and blood pressure detector will have to wait for all of the standard regulatory approval, so I’ll hold off judgment accordingly.
 Samsung’s certainly not attempting to price competitively here. At $400 and $430 for the for the 41mm and 45mm versions, it’s a premium price tag. It’s clear that the company doesn’t see companies like Huawei or even Fitbit as its primary competitors. As with its flagship smartphones, Samsung’s got Apple firmly in its sights, and it’s priced to match. Apple is far and away still the best option for iOS users, but when it comes to Android, not many can compete with Samsung’s premium offering.
title: 三星Galaxy Watch 3回顾

note:
自SpaceX首次亮相以来，私人太空发射行业已经大幅扩张，但一些市场，如印度，刚刚获得监管支持，为私人参与者的参与扫清了道路。然而，现在这些障碍正在消除，私人发射初创公司Skyroot正在飞跃成为该国第一家私人太空运载火箭制造商，通过新的、成功的上级发动机燃烧测试取得了重大的技术进步。
Skyroot是两年前由一个团队创立的，其中包括曾在印度空间研究组织(ISRO)工作的火箭工程师，包括首席执行官兼联合创始人帕万·库马尔·钱达纳(Pawan Kumar Chandana)。到目前为止，这家初创公司已经筹集了430万美元，其中包括来自太空和国防承包商太阳能工业公司(Solar Industries)的资金，目前它正在再次进行融资过程，希望在2021年之前再获得1500万美元。
在技术方面，Skyroot目前正专注于开发其第一枚运载火箭“Vikram-I”，该运载火箭正在制造过程中，并有望在2021年12月左右进行首次发射。上级发动机绰号为“拉曼”，以印度物理学家和诺贝尔奖获得者C.V.拉曼的名字命名，此次成功试射是验证这一关键部件的重要一步，一旦Vikram-I投入运行，它将处理将任何有效载荷卫星最终插入其目标轨道的工作。
这也是Skyroot整体火箭制造技术的关键一步，因为它代表着其3D打印推进剂喷射器的完全合格，该公司表示，这将发动机的质量降低了50%，降低了建造所需的零部件，并将制造周期缩短了80%。
Skyroot的下一步是正在开发的火箭的两个完整阶段的试射，将在接下来的六个月内进行。该公司还同时在研发下一代运载火箭Vikram-2和Vikram-3，这两种运载火箭将在2022-2023年左右上市，与包括SpaceX在内的私营公司提供的现有更大的拼车火箭相比，具有成本竞争力。
note_en:
The private space launch industry has expanded significantly since the debut of  SpaceX,   but some markets, like India, are just now getting the regulatory support to clear the way for private players to participate. Now that those barriers are coming down, however, private launch startup  Skyroot is leaping ahead on its way to becoming the first private space launch vehicle maker in the country, making significant technical progress with a new, successful upper stage engine burn test.
 Skyroot was founded two years ago by a team that includes rocket engineers who previously worked at the Indian Space Research Organization (ISRO), including CEO and co-founder Pawan Kumar Chandana. The startup has raised $4.3 million to date, including from space and defence contractor Solar Industries, and it’s currently going through the funding process again in hopes of securing another $15 million by 2021.
  On the technical side, Skyroot is currently focused on developing its very first launch vehicle, the ‘Vikram-I,” which is in the process of being manufactured and is on track for its first launch sometime around December 2021. This successful test fire of the upper stage engine, which is nickname ‘Raman’ after Indian physicist and Nobel prize winner C.V. Raman, is an important step in validating this key component, which will handle the final insertion of any payload satellites into their target orbit once Vikram-I is operational.
 This is also a key step for Skyroot’s overall rocket building technology, since it represents full qualification of its 3D-printed propellant injector, which the company says reduces the mass of the engine by 50 percent, and drops the components required in its construction, as well as its lead time for manufacturing by 80 percent.
 Next up for Skyroot are test fires of two full stages of its rocket under development, to take place over the course of the next six months. The company is also concurrently already at work on Vikram-2 and Vikram-3, next-generation launch vehicles that are set to follow in terms of availability sometime around 2022-2023, and offer cost-competitiveness with existing, larger rideshare rockets already available form private companies including  SpaceX  .
title: 印度第一家私人太空发射初创公司Skyroot成功进行上级发动机起火测试
title_en: India’s first private space launch startup Skyroot succeeds with upper stage engine fire test

note:
现在，除了自3月份以来积累的数百万在线粉丝外，曾在谷歌工作的牙买加美国人库珀(Cooper)还获得了Netflix的喜剧特辑。
名为“莎拉·库珀：一切都好”(Sarah Cooper：Everything‘s Fine)的编剧兼制片人娜塔莎·里昂(Natasha Lyonne)将执导这部作品，并将由喜剧演员玛雅·鲁道夫(Maya Rudolph)担任执行制片人(她本人也将越来越忙，重新扮演参议员卡马拉·哈里斯(Kamala Harris)在“周六夜现场”中的角色)。
这只是库珀帽子上最新也是最大的羽毛，她和丈夫住在纽约，她开玩笑地说，丈夫对她的特朗普蠢货已经失去了耐心。正如她今年夏天告诉“名利场”(Vanity Fair)的那样，他“不得不一遍又一遍地听到唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)的声音”。“我想他可能会在某个时候跳窗。”
库珀最近还与经纪公司WME签约，6月份与吉米·法伦(Jimmy Fallon)一起出现在“今夜秀”(The Tonight Show)中，昨天，甚至还客串了这档传奇节目。
根据VF的采访，库珀在电视上的其他抱负包括写一部关于一位过于自信的老板的节目，他“到处都是乱七八糟的东西，但不知何故还是失败了。”
目前还不清楚Netflix的这一特别节目是否让人发痒，但毫无疑问，许多特朗普的批评者-以及一些支持者-将密切关注它的特点。
Netflix目前还没有分享太多，但它表示，这部作品将是一部综艺特辑，其各种小插曲涉及“政治、种族、性别、阶级和其他轻松的主题”。
note_en:
Now, in addition to the millions of online followers she has amassed since March, Cooper — a Jamaican American who once worked at  Google   — has landed a  Netflix   comedy special.
 Titled “Sarah Cooper: Everything’s Fine,” the writer and producer Natasha Lyonne is set to direct the production, and it will be executive produced by the comic-actor Maya Rudolph (who will herself be increasingly busy,  reprising her role on “Saturday Night Live” as Senator Kamala Harris).
  It’s just the latest — and biggest — feather in the cap of Cooper, who lives in New York with a husband whom she has kiddingly described as running out of patience with her Trump schtick. He “has to hear Donald Trump’s voice over and over again,” as she  told Vanity Fair this summer. “I think he’s probably going to jump out the window at some point.”
 Cooper also recently signed with the talent agency WME, appeared on “The Tonight Show” with Jimmy Fallon in June, and yesterday, even  guest-hosted the storied show.
 According to that VF interview, Cooper’s other TV ambitions include writing a show about an overly confident boss who “fucks up all over the place and still somehow fails up.”
 It isn’t clear if this Netflix special scratches that itch, but no doubt plenty of Trump detractors — and some supporters — will be keeping an eye out to see what it  does feature.
 Netflix isn’t sharing much yet, but it says the production will be a variety special whose various vignettes deal “with issues of politics, race, gender, class, and other light subjects.”
title: 莎拉·库珀(Sarah Cooper)-以在TikTok上对唐纳德·特朗普(Donald Trump)的印象而闻名-刚刚获得了一笔Netflix交易
title_en: Sarah Cooper — known for her impressions of Donald Trump on TikTok — just landed a Netflix deal

note:
上周分享的优步自己的业绩，为Lyft的未来提供了线索。结果是：叫车业务大幅减少。
Lyft公布第二季度营收为3.393亿美元，每股收益为负1.41美元。根据雅虎财经的平均数据，分析师此前预计该公司公布的收入为3.3677亿美元，每股亏损0.99美元。
与一年前的业绩相比，Lyft的季度业绩一团糟。营收从本季度的8.673亿美元下降至2.658亿美元，降幅为61%，比去年同期调整后的净亏损1.973亿美元还要糟糕。调整后的EBITDA从2019年第二季度的2.041亿美元亏损下滑至公司最近一个季度的2.803亿美元赤字。
值得注意的是，与去年同期相比，Lyft确实改善了GAAP净亏损，尽管该公司在最近几个季度强调了其他盈利指标。
为什么Lyft的股价在盘后交易中走高？因为投资者知道Lyft的季度是冲销的。相反，他们寻找的是一份回归表格，以及一张关于公司在期末手头有多少现金的便条。最重要的是，Lyft回答了这两个问题，称第二季度“有28亿美元的无限制现金、现金等价物和短期投资”，“与4月份相比，7月份的月度拼车次数增加了78%。”
尽管Lyft 2020年前两个季度的运营现金流达到了令人震惊的9.586亿美元，但这足以让该公司从公开市场获得提振。
在我们结束之前，在我们阅读报告时，还有一些其他的小细节：
裁员代价高昂。根据Lyft的说法，公司在第二季度看到“2020年第二季度的重组费用包括3210万美元的遣散费和相关的员工福利成本”。
但只有一点点。在同一段落的晚些时候，Lyft写道，它“产生了一项基于股票的补偿福利，主要与之前确认的4980万美元的未归属奖励的基于股票的补偿费用的冲销有关，导致该期间的净重组福利为1450万美元”。
债务并不总是坏账。截至2020年6月30日，Lyft扣除当前部分后有6.234亿美元的长期债务。今年早些时候，该公司以最低的货币利率筹集了一批债务，在这段业务期间给了它更多的缓冲。
我们正在密切关注该公司的股价，并深入调查乘客人数。还会有更多。
note_en:
Uber’s    own results, which were shared last week, provided hints of what was coming for Lyft. The upshot: a  sharply reduced ride-hailing business.
 In the second quarter,  Lyft   reported revenue of $339.3 million and earnings per share of negative $1.41. Analysts had anticipated the company reporting revenues of $336.77 million, and a per-share loss of $0.99, according to Yahoo Finance averages.
   Compared to its year-ago results, Lyft’s quarter was a mess. Revenue fell from $867.3 million, or 61%, in the quarter, and its adjusted net loss in Q2 came to $265.8 million, worse than its year-ago adjusted net loss of $197.3 million. Adjusted EBITDA slipped from a $204.1 million loss in Q2 2019 to a deficit of $280.3 million in the company’s most recent quarter.
 Notably, Lyft did improve its GAAP net loss compared to the year-ago period, though the company has stressed other profitability metrics in recent quarters.
  Why are Lyft shares higher in after-hours trading? Because investors knew that Lyft’s quarter was a write-off. What they were looking for instead was a return-to-form, and a note on how much cash the company had on hand at the end of the period. Up top, Lyft answered both questions, saying that it wrapped Q2 with “with $2.8 billion of unrestricted cash, cash equivalents and short-term investments,” and that “monthly rideshare rides in July were up 78% compared to April.”
 That’s enough for the company to get a jolt from the public markets, despite Lyft’s operating cash flow for the first two quarters of 2020 coming to a stunning -$958.6 million.
 Before we close, some other bits and bobs that stood out while we were reading the report:
 Laying off people is expensive. Per Lyft, the company saw “restructuring charges in the second quarter of 2020 included $32.1 million of severance and related employee benefit costs” in Q2.
 But only kinda. Later in the same paragraph, Lyft wrote that it “incurred a stock-based compensation benefit primarily related to the reversal of previously recognized stock-based compensation expenses for unvested awards of $49.8 million, resulting in a net restructuring benefit of $14.5 million” in the period.
 Debt isn’t always bad. As of June 30, 2020, Lyft has $623.4 million in long-term debt, net of current portion. The company raised a  bunch of debt earlier this year at rock-bottom monetary rates, giving it more cushion during this period of business.
 We’re keeping tabs on the company’s share price and digging into rider numbers. More to come.
title: Lyft股价小幅上涨，此前公布第二季度营收下降61%
title_en: Lyft shares get small bump after reporting 61% Q2 revenue decline

note:
全球对Zoom以外的解决方案的学习需求，正是总部位于哥本哈根的初创公司Labster迅速增长的原因。Labster是一家总部位于哥本哈根的初创公司，帮助个人使用虚拟现实参与STEM实验室场景。自3月份以来，Labster的VR产品的使用量增加了15倍。
紧随这一前所未有的势头，拉伯斯特加入了正在筹集资金的教育科技初创公司的行列，并宣布已获得900万美元的股权风险融资。这轮融资由GGV牵头，现有投资者Owl Ventures、Balderton和Northzone参与其中。
首席执行官兼联合创始人迈克尔·延森在接受TechCrunch采访时表示：“新冠肺炎是Labster的一位伟大的意识建设者，让老师们看到了在线学习的好处，而不是在很大程度上失败的只缩放学习。”
Labster销售其电子学习解决方案，以支持和增强面对面课程。根据机构选择的订阅，参与者可以获得不同程度的虚拟实验室访问权限。想象一下一系列的实验，从了解细菌的生长和分离到探索系外行星的生物多样性。除了每个模拟，Labster还提供特定概念的3D动画、模拟的重播、测验问题和虚拟学习助手。
虽然Labster的大多数客户都是私立机构，但在大流行期间，该公司与加州所有的社区大学达成了一项协议。这一合作伙伴关系为Labster的客户群增加了210万名学生，詹森表示，年度许可交易和合作伙伴关系的更广泛增长支撑了这一基础。
随着GGV的加入，Labster正寻求加强在亚洲的地位。打入新市场通常需要战略投资者关注市场的运作方式，思考，最重要的是学习。亚洲市场对教育科技公司来说尤其有利可图，因为与北美市场相比，消费者支出更高。
Lee表达了对自动化、虚拟和基于AI的教师如何帮助弥合K-12市场与各地缺乏高质量教师之间的差距的兴趣。
Jensen表示，这笔资金还将用于支持该公司的移动服务，因为与北美和欧洲市场相比，亚洲市场的移动使用率很高。
这轮融资明显小于Labster之前于2019年4月完成的2100万美元的B系列融资。这与Master Class、Coursera和据报道Udemy等科技公司筹集九位数融资的势头形成了鲜明对比。
詹森说，900万美元的支票是引入GGV的战略增长支票(Labster的所有现有投资者也参与了这轮融资)。自2012年成立以来，该公司在筹集资金方面一直相对保守。到目前为止，包括本轮融资在内，Labster已经筹集了4000万美元的风险投资。
因此，他辩称，新资金是进攻性资本，而不是防御性资本。这是打开一扇全球大门的战略检查。
在冠状病毒大流行期间，这不是第一家教育科技公司筹集的融资规模低于预期。今年4月，教育科技独角兽多林戈(Duolingo)筹集了1000万美元，用于向亚洲扩张，并引入通用大西洋(General Atlantic)作为投资者，向全球市场扩张。
然而，Duolingo的现金流为正。詹森没有评论Labster是否已经盈利，但补充说，这是一轮“重大的上涨”，使该公司的估值超过1亿美元。
“我们的首要目标仍然是快速增长和全球影响力，而不是利润，”他告诉TechCrunch。
note_en:
The global need for learning solutions beyond Zoom is precisely why  Labster, a Copenhagen-based startup that helps individuals engage in STEM lab scenarios using virtual reality, is growing rapidly. Since March, the usage of  Labster’s   VR product has increased 15X.
 On the heels of this unprecedented momentum, Labster joins a chorus of edtech startups raising right now, and announced it has brought on $9 million in equity venture funding. The round was led by GGV, with participation from existing investors Owl Ventures, Balderton and Northzone.
 “COVID-19 has been a great awareness builder of  Labster, opening teachers’ eyes to the good sides of online learning as opposed to  Zoom-only learning, which is largely failing,” CEO and co-founder Michael Jensen told TechCrunch.
 Labster sells its e-learning solution to support and enhance in-person courses. Based on the subscription an institution chooses, participants can get differing degrees of access to a virtual laboratory. Imagine a range of experiments, from understanding bacterial growth and isolation to exploring the biodiversity of an exoplanet. Along with each simulation, Labster offers 3D animations for certain concepts, re-plays of simulations, quiz questions and a virtual learning assistant.
  While the majority of Labster’s customers are private institutions, the company landed a deal with all of California’s community colleges during the pandemic. The partnership added  2.1 million students to Labster’s customer base, which Jensen said has been bolstered by a broader growth in annual license deals and partnerships.
 With GGV on board, Labster is looking to strengthen position in Asia. Breaking into new markets often requires a strategic investor with eyes on the ground on how that market works, thinks and, most importantly, learns. Asian markets are specifically lucrative for edtech companies because consumer spend is higher compared to the North American market.
  Lee has  expressed interest in how automation, virtual and AI-based teachers can help bridge the gap between K-12 markets and lack of good-quality teachers everywhere.
 Jensen said that the capital will also be used to bolster the company’s mobile offering, since Asian markets have high mobile usage compared to North American and European markets.
 The round is significantly smaller than Labster’s previous $21 million Series B, closed in April of 2019. And it contrasts sharply to the momentum that has benefited edtech companies like MasterClass, Coursera and, reportedly, Udemy into raising nine-figure rounds.
  Jensen says that the $9 million check was a strategic growth check to bring on GGV (all existing investors in Labster also participated in the round). Since being founded in 2012, the company has been relatively conservative in raising cash. To date, inclusive of this round, Labster has raised $40 million in venture capital.
 He argues the new money, thus, is offensive capital instead of defensive capital. It’s a strategic check to open a global door.
 This isn’t the first time an edtech company has raised a smaller round than expected during the coronavirus pandemic. In April, edtech unicorn Duolingo  raised a short $10 million to expand into Asia and bring on General Atlantic as an investor to expand into global markets.
 Duolingo, however, is cash-flow positive. Jensen did not comment on if Labster has turned a profit, but adds that it was a “significant up round” that brought the company’s valuation to above $100 million.
 “Our primary objectives continue to be rapid growth and global impact, not profits,” he told TechCrunch.
title: Labster获得新资金，将其虚拟现实科学实验室软件带到亚洲
title_en: Labster lands new cash to bring its virtual reality science lab software to Asia

note:
Dropbox今天将正式推出测试版的几个新的消费者功能，以及一些面向企业的新工具。
这家云存储巨头在6月份首次推出了密码管理器-配备了一款适用于Android和iOS的独立移动应用程序。与市场上的其他密码管理应用程序类似，Dropbox Password存储和加密用户的在线密码，并在所有设备(台式机和移动设备)之间同步这些密码，因此用户不必记住多个登录凭据。
Dropbox密码还可以为您的在线服务(如Facebook、Twitter、Netflix和Dropbox本身)建议强的、随机生成的个人密码。
Dropbox Password是去年收购总部位于马萨诸塞州的Valt的结果，Valt在与Dropbox整合之前迅速关闭了自己的应用程序。从今天开始，每个人都可以通过Dropbox Plus或专业订阅获得Dropbox密码。
这家总部位于旧金山的公司还将于今天正式推出其之前宣布的计算机备份功能。该工具可供Dropbox Basic、Plus和Professional用户使用，可自动为存储在PC或Mac上的任何文件夹创建基于云的备份，并可持续同步。
Dropbox今天还推出了新的Vault功能，以帮助用户共享对特定文件的访问权限，如护照扫描或敏感的税务文件，同时确保这些文件在PIN码后的安全。Vault文件夹与Dropbox上的其他文件和文件夹一起位于Dropbox上，尽管它只能从Dropbox.com或Dropbox移动应用程序打开-Dropbox Vault中包含的文件不会本地存储在用户的桌面上。Dropbox Vault从今天开始对所有Dropbox Plus订阅者开放。
除了更多以消费者为中心的功能外，Dropbox今天还推出了Dropbox Professional订阅计划独有的两个功能。“品牌共享”允许用户使用他们的名字和品牌定制他们共享的文件和文档。
最后，Dropbox为专业订户提供了一项新的“流量和洞察力”功能，允许他们跟踪与他们共享的文件和文件夹的所有互动，包括谁点击了链接，他们使用的是什么设备，何时访问，以及他们是否查看或下载了内容。
note_en:
Dropbox is officially launching a handful of new consumer features out of beta today, along with some new tools for businesses.
 The cloud storage giant first introduced its password manager — replete with a standalone mobile app  for Android and  iOS —  back in June. Similar to other password management apps on the market, Dropbox Passwords stores and encrypts users’ online passwords and syncs them across all devices (desktop and mobile) so users don’t have to remember multiple login credentials.
  Dropbox Passwords can also suggest strong, randomly generated, individual passwords for your online services, such as Facebook, Twitter, Netflix, and Dropbox itself.
  Dropbox Passwords is the result of last year’s  acquisition of Massachusetts-based Valt, which swiftly shuttered its own apps ahead of integration with Dropbox. Dropbox Passwords is available to everyone on a Dropbox Plus or Professional subscription from today.
 The San Francisco-based company is also launching its previously announced computer backup feature in general availability today. The tool, which is available for Dropbox Basic, Plus, and Professional users, automatically creates a cloud-based backup of any folder stored on a PC or Mac and is continuously synced.
  Dropbox also launched its new Vault feature in general availability today to help users share access to specific files — such as passport scans or sensitive tax documents — while keeping those documents secure behind a PIN code. The Vault folder sits alongside other files and folders on Dropbox, though it can only be opened from Dropbox.com or the Dropbox mobile app — files contained in Dropbox Vault are not stored locally on a user’s desktop. Dropbox Vault is available to all Dropbox Plus subscribers from today.
  Alongside the more consumer-centric features, Dropbox today unveiled two features exclusive to the Dropbox Professional subscription plan. “Branded sharing” allows users to customize the files and documents they share with their name and branding.
  Finally, Dropbox is giving Professional subscribers access to a new “traffic and insights” feature, allowing them to track all the interactions with files and folders that they share, including who clicked on the link, what device they were using, when they accessed it, and whether they viewed or downloaded the content.
title: Dropbox推出密码管理器、计算机备份和Beta版的安全“Vaults”
title_en: Dropbox Launches Password Manager, Computer Backup, and Secure 'Vaults' Out of Beta

note:
鱿鱼是最聪明的海洋生物之一。与章鱼和墨鱼等其他会喷墨的头足类动物一样，鱿鱼拥有所有无脊椎动物中最大的大脑。它们还有一个极其复杂的神经系统，能够瞬间伪装自己的身体，并使用各种信号相互交流。
长期以来，科学家们一直对这些复杂的行为感到惊讶，并试图理解为什么这些有触角的生物如此聪明。基因编辑或许能帮助研究人员揭开头足类动物大脑的神秘面纱。但到目前为止，要做到这一点太难了--部分原因是头足类动物的胚胎受到坚硬的外层的保护，这使得操纵它们变得困难。
最近，一组海洋科学家使用DNA编辑工具CRISPR成功地设计出了第一只经过基因改造的鱿鱼。除了是生物学上的一大里程碑，这一进展对人类健康也有潜在的影响：因为头足类动物的大脑很大，所以它们被用来研究阿尔茨海默氏症和帕金森氏症等神经退行性疾病。
编辑这些动物的基因的能力可以帮助科学家研究与学习和记忆有关的基因，以及特定的头足类动物行为。马萨诸塞州伍兹霍尔海洋生物实验室的资深科学家、第一只转基因鱿鱼的主要设计师约书亚·罗森塔尔博士告诉OneZero：“我认为你将看到神经生物学家对这些(基因编辑的)有机体的使用出现了巨大的跃升。”乔舒亚·罗森塔尔博士是马萨诸塞州伍兹霍尔海洋生物实验室的资深科学家，也是第一只转基因鱿鱼的关键架构师。
罗森塔尔和他的同事们使用CRISPR剪掉了一种导致鱿鱼皮肤着色的基因。因此，编辑后的鱿鱼是透明的，而不是通常的微红色斑点。研究结果发表在7月30日的“当代生物学”杂志上。
但是，为什么要费心去创造一条无色的鱿鱼呢？罗森塔尔说，色素沉着基因是进行实验的合乎逻辑的起点。“如果你看到色素沉着消失，就很容易看出基因编辑是否起作用了，”他解释说。能够修补头足类DNA将使科学家能够在非常基础的水平上更好地研究他们的单个基因的作用。
取得这样的成就并不容易。科学家们已经成功地制造了基因编辑的小鼠、猴子和其他研究动物，以帮助它们研究一系列行为和医疗条件。但到目前为止，他们还没有成功地操纵头足类动物的基因。
首先，科学家首先需要一张鱿鱼基因组图，以便在其DNA中找到他们想要编辑的确切位置。鱿鱼基因组是最近才完成的，尽管它还没有发表在同行评议的期刊上。研究人员还需要一种在实验室创造鱿鱼胚胎并在不造成损害的情况下对其进行编辑的方法。
该研究的合著者、马里兰圣玛丽学院研究动物胚胎的发育生物学家Karen Crawford博士想出了一种方法，将雌性鱿鱼的卵子与雄性鱿鱼的精子在适当的条件下混合，形成胚胎。
然后，研究小组必须找出如何将CRISPR系统注射到胚胎中。鱿鱼胚胎的涂层使针头很难穿透它们。当研究小组试图注射胚胎时，他们的针头不断断裂。克劳福德开发了一把微型剪刀，在涂层上剪下一个小洞，让特制的石英针进入里面。这样做特别棘手：一个太大的洞可能会导致胚胎渗出。但使用克劳福德的技术，他们成功地将CRISPR注射到受精后不久的胚胎中，而没有任何损害。
研究人员使用了一种名为长鳍近岸鱿鱼的物种，这种鱿鱼每年春天成群结队地迁徙到科德角附近的水域。几十年来，科学家们长途跋涉来到科德角西南端的伍兹霍尔，收集和研究这些动物。对它们的研究导致了关于神经冲动的突破性发现，并获得了1963年的诺贝尔奖。
但是长鳍近岸鱿鱼不能在实验室里活很长时间，因为它们长得太大了。在未来，研究人员将尝试将他们的基因编辑技术用于更容易在水箱中种植的较小种类的鱿鱼。
他们还想使用CRISPR来追踪鱿鱼的神经活动。这项技术可以用来插入一种所谓的“报告”基因，这种基因可以制造一种荧光蛋白，当神经系统处于电活动时，这种荧光蛋白就会发光。“这是一种有着复杂行为和大量神经细胞的有机体，”罗森塔尔说。“如果能够观察这些神经细胞的活动，一次观察很多神经细胞，试图将行为与活动联系起来，那就太好了。”
如果他们能做到这一点，科学家们就可以研究这些动物不可思议的伪装能力所涉及的大脑结构。鱿鱼利用其特殊的视力和一种名为色团的皮肤细胞几乎瞬间改变它们的颜色以躲避捕食者。这些特化细胞与神经系统相连。
罗森塔尔说：“头足类动物有一个奇怪而疯狂的身体计划。”“它们看起来不像任何其他生物。”科学家们也对这些动物灵活手臂上的吸盘着迷。这些吸盘可以感知它们的环境，并处理各种感官信息--本质上是让头足类动物用手臂“思考”。
但由于它们的高级智商，通过基因操作这些动物会带来伦理问题。在加拿大和欧洲，头足类的研究受到严格监管，但在美国，没有这样的保护措施。海洋生物实验室已就头足类动物在研究中的伦理和人道使用提出了自己的指导方针。
科学家芭芭拉·金(Barbara King)和洛里·马里诺(Lori Marino)在2019年的《动物感知》(Animal Sensence)杂志上撰文称，科学家在使用这些动物进行研究时，应该考虑对它们的治疗。他们说：“具有讽刺意味的是，大多数对章鱼进行研究的研究人员指出，章鱼的庞大、复杂和复杂的大脑是他们想要研究章鱼的原因，完全忽略了一个事实，那就是这可能正是应该让我们犹豫的原因。”
随着研究人员开始改变这些动物的遗传密码，他们将需要考虑它们应该走多远。
note_en:
Squid are among the smartest ocean dwellers. Along with other ink-squirting cephalopods like octopuses and cuttlefish, squid boast the largest brains of all invertebrates. They also have an incredibly complex nervous system capable of instantaneously camouflaging their bodies and communicating with each other using various signals.
 Scientists have long marveled at these sophisticated behaviors and have tried to understand why these tentacled creatures are so intelligent. Gene editing may be able to help researchers unravel the mysteries of the cephalopod brain. But until now, it’s been too hard to do—in part because cephalopod embryos are protected by a hard outer layer that makes manipulating them difficult.
 Recently, a group of marine scientists managed to engineer the first genetically altered squid using the DNA editing tool CRISPR. In addition to being a big milestone in biology, the advance has potential implications for human health: Because of their big brains, cephalopods are used to study neurodegenerative diseases like Alzheimer’s and Parkinson’s.
 The ability to edit the genes of these animals could help scientists study the genes involved in learning and memory as well as specific cephalopod behaviors. “I think you’re going to see a huge jump in the use of these [gene-edited] organisms by neurobiologists,” Joshua Rosenthal, PhD, a senior scientist at the Marine Biological Laboratory in Woods Hole, Massachusetts, and a key architect of the first genetically engineered squid, tells  OneZero.
 Rosenthal and his colleagues used CRISPR to snip out a gene responsible for the coloring of the squid’s skin. As a result, the edited squid were transparent instead of having their usual reddish spots. The results were published July 30 in the journal   Current Biology.
 But why bother to create a colorless squid? Rosenthal says the pigmentation gene was a logical starting place for experimentation. “If you see the pigmentation go away, it’s easy to see if the gene editing is working,” he explains. Being able to tinker with cephalopod DNA will allow scientists to better study what their individual genes do at a very basic level.
 The accomplishment wasn’t easy. Scientists have successfully made gene-edited mice, monkeys, and other research animals to help them study a range of behaviors and medical conditions. But until now, they hadn’t been successful at manipulating the genes of cephalopods.
 For one thing, scientists first needed a map of the squid’s genome to find the precise place in its DNA that they wanted to edit. The squid genome was only recently completed, though it hasn’t been published in a peer-reviewed journal yet. Researchers also needed a way to create squid embryos in the lab and edit them without causing damage.
 Co-author Karen Crawford, PhD, a developmental biologist at St. Mary’s College of Maryland who studies animal embryos, figured out a way to mix eggs from a female squid and sperm from a male squid in the appropriate conditions to form embryos.
  Then, the team had to figure out how to inject the CRISPR system into the embryos. The squid embryos’ coating makes it difficult to penetrate them with a needle. When the team tried injecting the embryos, their needles kept breaking. Crawford developed a pair of microscissors to snip a tiny hole in the coating to allow a specially-made quartz needle to get inside. Doing so was especially tricky: A hole that’s too big can cause the embryo to ooze out. But using Crawford’s technique, they successfully injected CRISPR into the embryos soon after fertilization without any damage.
 The researchers used a species called the longfin inshore squid, which migrates to the waters off Cape Cod every spring in droves. For decades, scientists have trekked to Woods Hole, on the southwest tip of Cape Cod, to collect and study these animals. Research on them led to breakthrough discoveries about nerve impulses that won the  Nobel Prize in 1963.
 But longfin inshore squid can’t live long in a lab because they get too big. In the future, the researchers will try using their gene-editing technique on smaller species of squid that can be more easily grown in tanks.
 They also want to use CRISPR to track the squid’s neural activity. The technique can be used to insert a so-called “reporter” gene, which makes a fluorescent protein that lights up when the nervous system is electrically active. “This is an organism with sophisticated behaviors and a lot of nerve cells,” Rosenthal says. “It would be nice to be able to look at the activity of those nerve cells, lots of them at a time to try to correlate behavior with activity.”
 If they can do so, scientists could study the brain structure involved in the incredible camouflage ability of these animals. Squid use their exceptional eyesight and a type of skin cell called the chromatophores to almost instantaneously change their color to hide from predators. These specialized cells are connected to the nervous system.
 “Cephalopods have a weird, crazy body plan,” Rosenthal says. “They don’t look like any other organism.” Scientists are also fascinated by the suckers that line the animals’ flexible arms. These suckers can sense their environment and process all sorts of sensory information — essentially allowing cephalopods to “think” with their arms.
 But because of their advanced intelligence, genetically manipulating these animals comes with ethical questions. In Canada and Europe, research on cephalopods is highly regulated, but in the United States, there are no such protections. For its part, the Marine Biological Laboratory has come up with its own guidelines on the ethical and humane use of cephalopods in research.
 Writing in the journal   Animal Sentience in 2019, scientists Barbara King and Lori Marino argued that scientists should consider the treatment of these animals when using them for research. “Ironically, most researchers who conduct studies of octopuses point to their large, complex and sophisticated brains as reasons for wanting to study them, completely overlooking the fact that this might be the very reason that should give us pause,” they said.
 As researchers begin to alter the genetic code of these animals, they will need to consider how far they should go.
title: 历史上第一个基因编辑鱿鱼是生物学上的突破
title_en: The First Gene-Edited Squid in History Is a Biological Breakthrough

note:
随着反对警察杀害乔治·弗洛伊德的抗议活动开始进行，有报道开始流传，称神秘的黑客组织匿名者组织卷土重来。
谣言始于一段视频，视频中一名身着黑衣的人戴着该组织标志性的盖伊·福克斯(Guy Fawkes)面具。“问候，美国公民，”这个人物用一种令人毛骨悚然、扭曲的声音说。“这是”匿名者“给明尼阿波利斯警察局的信息。”蒙面播音员谈到了弗洛伊德被杀事件和警察行为不端的更大模式，得出结论说：“我们将向世界揭露你们的许多罪行。我们是军团。期待我们的到来。“。
这段视频重新引发了人们对“匿名者”的热情，特别是在年轻人中。与该组织相关的Twitter账户出现了一批新的追随者，其中有几个人达到了数百万人。
在2012年人气最高的时候，匿名者是一个由数千名活动人士组成的网络，其中少数人是黑客，致力于个人自由的左翼自由主义理想，反对巩固企业和政府的权力。但在一连串的逮捕之后，它基本上已经从人们的视线中消失了。
现在，新一代的人渴望加入。“如何申请成为”匿名者“的一部分？我只是想帮忙，我甚至会给黑客做咖啡或糖果。“一位英国活动家在推特上开玩笑说，获得了数十万个赞和转发。
匿名“斯坦”(超级粉丝)账号在TikTok上重新混音了这段视频，为这位影子人物增添了迷人的指甲和珠宝。其他人则使用聊天服务Discord创建虚拟空间，在那里，数千名新的奉献者可以用模因和同人小说来庆祝黑客。推特上最大的匿名账号之一恳求人们“停止给我们发裸体照片”。
视频发布后，一系列黑客攻击接踵而至。新闻媒体猜测，是“匿名者”在5月30日和31日劫持了芝加哥警方的扫描仪，播放N.W.A.的“操他妈的警察”和泰·佐纳迪(Tay Zonday)的“巧克力雨”，这首歌是2007年的一首歌，是该乐队的非官方国歌。同样，当明尼阿波利斯警察局的网站因一次明显的DDoS攻击而下线时-这是一次让目标网站流量不堪重负的黑客攻击-社交媒体被归功于匿名。
三周后的6月19日，一个匿名身份的人泄露了来自全美200多个机构的数百兆字节的警方内部文件。这起名为#BlueLeaks的黑客攻击几乎没有关于警察不当行为的信息。然而，它确实揭示了地方和联邦执法团体在5月和6月的骚乱期间向明尼苏达州警察传播研究不足和夸大的错误信息，并努力监控抗议者的社交媒体活动。
我最近出版了一本书，详细描述了匿名者的错综复杂的起源，直到上个月，我还以为这个团体已经消失了。我对它的重新出现感到惊讶，我想知道它是如何以及为什么会卷土重来的，首先是谁制作了这段新的视频。没过多久我就发现了。
，这不是匿名者的特征。这一标记在复印件中模糊不清，但在原始帖子中以白色字体出现：“anonews.co。”这个URL把我带到了一个新闻聚合网站，它把我带到了该网站的Facebook页面，视频的第一次迭代是在5月28日发布的。一家名为MiDialab Ltd.的英国公司控制着该页面。我给页面上列出的电子邮件写了信，公司老板当天就回复了。这位人士要求匿名，但愿意让我与视频的创作者联系。
我怀疑我是在追赶某个俄罗斯巨魔农场的尾巴，该农场的业务是促进各种类型的激进分裂。5月29日，俄罗斯国有媒体RT率先报道了这段视频。那数以百万计的新追随者蜂拥至匿名推特账户呢？正如这些账户本身指出的那样，许多都是机器人。
收到电子邮件后不到一个小时，我就接到了从马里兰州哈福德县郊区打来的电话，就在我住的地方以北。电话里的那个人告诉我他的名字是约翰·维贝斯。“嘿，伙计，”他说。“惊讶我是本地人吗？这段视频是我拍的。“。
Vibes告诉我，在过去的十年里，他一直是一名政党推动者，在巴尔的摩和费城组织狂欢派对，这让他进入了反文化思想，并最终进入了激进主义。“我一直在写关于警察暴行的文章，运营anonews.co的人联系了我，他是一位英国的科技企业家，同意匿名者的政治观点，并希望支持它。”Vibes是一名自由撰稿人，他为Facebook页面撰写和制作视频，Facebook页面的功能是新闻中心。他说：“大多数情况下，我们只报道”匿名者“感兴趣的新闻--银行系统、腐败。”“每个月我们会看几次大新闻，我们会把总体情绪汇总成一段视频。”
事实上，Facebook页面定期发布匿名视频，其中许多视频是由Vibes制作的。但在最近的病毒式视频中，他并不是对着摄像机说话的蒙面人物。该页面经常重复使用相同的素材，而只是使用新的音频。
Vibes强调他不是黑客，而是一个在社交媒体和聊天室呼应匿名成员情绪的“记者”。Facebook页面的目的是为这条信息创建一个出口。“需要说明的是，我们不是俄罗斯的巨魔农场，”维贝斯说。
尽管如此，我和Vibes的谈话让我不确定“匿名者”是否真的回来了。5月和6月初的新黑客攻击在很大程度上是通过谣言与该组织联系在一起的。这段视频并不是由匿名黑客发布的，而是由一名支持他们信息的活动家发布的。从某种意义上说，Vibes只是另一个粉丝，混音了一种混音。这一切都只是烟雾弥漫吗？
但在过去的一个月里，当我与各种现任和前任匿名黑客交谈时，他们都坚称匿名确实在重新激活。要理解为什么，以及这到底意味着什么，记住对“匿名者”的两种相互竞争的解释是有帮助的。
从某种意义上说，匿名者是一个分散的科技活动家社区，他们在他们称之为“运营”的项目上以小团体的形式进行合作。
但是，还有第二个匿名的定义。匿名成员会告诉您，匿名者没有成员，它不是一个组，而是一个横幅。人们聚集在一起。就像海盗旗一样，任何人都可以举着它的桅杆，开始以“匿名者”的名义行事。
“是义务警员，”格雷格·豪什(Gregg Housh)告诉我，他是2008年匿名反山达基视频的创作者之一。“匿名者”就是专门为此而设计的。在最初的创立中，它以巨魔…的身份存在。人们想做什么就做什么，带着义务警员的暗示。它被设计成完全开放的。任何人都可以匿名。“。
在Vibes制作的新视频中，匿名者代表着法外正义，超级英雄进入了正常法律程序无法实现的权利-这个想法似乎非常有吸引力，因为在一些人看来，普通的司法执法者-警察-似乎是犯罪的根源。
我与“匿名者”有关联的线人都告诉我同样的事情：人们纷纷回到聊天室来协调新的“行动”。这就是匿名者一直以来的工作方式。一段病毒式的视频引发了一波热情。然后，群龙无首的集体辩论该怎么办。有时，它会以行为抗议行为了结，比如侵入警方扫描仪或短暂关闭网站。但正如BlueLeaks所发生的那样，更熟练的黑客往往会窃取和泄露旨在用实质性证据支持政治事业的文件。
然而，这些年来，这群人和这场运动都发生了变化。为了跟踪匿名者的发展轨迹，有必要了解整个项目是如何开始的：就像青少年开的一个玩笑。
奥布里·科特尔(Aubrey Cottle)是一群网络恶作剧者中的一员，他们自称是“巨魔”，并在两个无政府主义的在线留言板上运行：“可怕的东西”和“4chan”。这些论坛上有成千上万的用户-几乎都是年轻人-但其中有一个更坚定的乐队，他们在同一个聊天室闲逛，在网上不和，在现实生活中相遇。他们自称无名氏。这个名字来源于4chan呈现用户名的方式。如果未指定，则默认情况下站点显示“匿名”。
2007年，一名男子出现在科特尔的门口。科特尔当时20岁，仍然和他的母亲住在多伦多。正如科特尔讲述的那样(部分得到了他的一位朋友的证实)，这名男子来自加拿大安全情报局(Canada Security Intelligence Service)，相当于美国的中央情报局(CIA)。出于好奇，科特尔把他带到自己的房间，房间里散落着硬盘、服务器设备和旧的90年代黑客杂志2600。
“你愿意用你的能力打击基地组织和恐怖组织吗？”经纪人问他。很多想法闪过科特尔的脑海：这个人是真的吗？我永远不会为联邦调查局工作。我应该删除所有内容吗？但最主要的是他觉得自己像个骗子。这个男人认为他是某种他不是的东西。
匿名巨魔喜欢在其他网站上进行“突袭”，用他们的“大军”用户淹没网络游戏和聊天室，扰乱空间。就像残忍的哥哥一样，他们经常选择他们能找到的最容易的目标-年幼的孩子。他们喜欢突袭一家名为哈博酒店(Habbo Hotel)的儿童游戏，将他们的头像排成一排，阻止人们进入在线游泳池。
当4chan开始打击组织突袭行动时，匿名者转移到了科特尔的模仿网站420chan，这个网站是他创建的，目的是讨论他的主要兴趣：毒品和职业摔跤。而科特尔成为了匿名者组织事实上的领导人，这是他喜欢的角色。科特尔告诉我，就是在这段时间里，他为这个团体制定了一套半开玩笑的规则，后来被称为臭名昭著的“互联网规则”。其中包括“3.我们是匿名者4.匿名者是军团5.匿名者永远不会原谅.”
科特尔和他的朋友也是第一批开始使用盖伊·福克斯面具的人。他们选择它仅仅是因为他们喜欢电影V for Vendetta，这是一部2005年改编自反乌托邦小说漫画书的电影。这部电影的主人公V穿上伪装，通过向建筑物投掷燃烧弹来对抗未来的法西斯警察国家，颠覆了最初的盖伊·福克斯(Guy Fawkes)的故事，他在英国民间传说中因试图炸毁1605年的议会而受到诽谤。
科特尔告诉CSIS，他会考虑它的提议(后来他拒绝了)，然后又回到了网络欺凌。但在当局来到科特尔的家门口后不久，“匿名者”就成了新闻人物。福克斯在洛杉矶的一家附属公司曾对该组织进行过一段报道，称他们是“服用类固醇的黑客”。这份报告暗示“匿名者”可能是一个恐怖组织，在片段的叙述上叠加了一辆面包车爆炸的库存镜头。
这个片段让“匿名者”很高兴。黑客是它的成员为了自己的娱乐而做的事情。现在，在媒体和政府的眼中，他们是一个阴暗而强大的阴谋集团，无所不能。这是人们想要相信他们的东西，是他们可以使用的东西。
“匿名者”花了2007年的大部分时间骚扰新纳粹主义电台主持人哈尔·特纳(Hal Turner)，不是因为该组织在这段时间完全是政治性的，而是因为特纳被证明是一个容易攻击的目标。每周，匿名者都会堵塞他的电话线，下载他的网站，或者向他家订购数百个披萨。但当它对特纳进行了如此彻底的黑客攻击，发现他是联邦调查局的线人时，乐趣突然结束了。
在特纳之后，“匿名者”需要一个新的目标。他们转向山达基教会，自20世纪90年代初以来，山达基教会一直是黑客和信息自由活动家的敌人。新行动的催化剂是一段由Housh制作的视频。它以福克斯新闻为灵感，暗示匿名者是一个强大的国际黑客团伙。“多年来，我们一直在关注你们，”它用文本到语音的计算机语音宣布。“我们的军团很多。”
当这段视频在网上疯传时，人们的热情达到了前所未有的高度。无名氏涌入他们曾经用来协调突袭的同一聊天室，这一次他们的号码被引导到世界各地主要城市一系列反对山达基的街头抗议活动中。(匿名指责山达基用伪科学欺骗其追随者，并非法压制批评者。)。数百人参加了我在纽约报道的一场抗议活动，几乎所有人都戴着盖伊·福克斯(Guy Fawkes)面具。
对于许多人来说，当他们意识到他们可以改变现实世界时，这种玩弄巨魔的愤世嫉俗被粉碎了。甚至让他们自己都感到惊讶的是，匿名者继承了一场自20世纪80年代以来一直在肆虐的冲突。一边是黑客，他们想把互联网作为个人赋权的工具；另一边是政府和企业，他们把互联网作为收集个人数据的万能筒。
目前，匿名运动分裂成相互竞争的巨魔和积极分子派别。科特尔率领着巨魔队，但他的队伍很快就失去了控制。
分水岭时刻出现在2010年底，当时支持朱利安·阿桑奇(Julian Assange)和维基解密(WikiLeaks)的匿名行动滚雪球般地演变成对贝宝(PayPal)和万事达卡(Mastercard)阻止维基解密捐款的大规模攻击。随着媒体的关注，数以千计的匿名者再次涌入聊天室，他们之前曾用来协调对电脑游戏的入侵，这一次是为了使公司网站瘫痪。
不久，“匿名者”揭露了安全公司HBGary Federal、科技监控巨头Palantir和私人安全公司Berico Technologies使用尼克松肮脏伎俩让维基解密难堪的计划。HBGary泄密事件成了头版新闻。“匿名者”的队伍更加壮大。
参与黑客攻击的匿名者组成了一个名为LulzSec(LOLS Security)的分支组织，并进行了一场高调的黑客狂欢，每当他们觉得索尼等大公司和几个政府机构践踏个人自由-或者只是为了表明他们可以这样做-就把这些组织作为攻击目标。但在2012年，联邦调查局逮捕了LulzSec的成员之一Hector“Sabu”Monsegur，他是一名28岁的男子，住在纽约市的公共住房里。萨布成为了一名线人，也是一次精心策划的诱捕行动的中心，这次行动导致该组织的许多主要参与者被捕。(Monsegur否认对这些逮捕负责，尽管并未否认自己是FBI线人。)。
匿名者从未完全康复。一小群无名氏留了下来，但旗帜背后的能量消散了。
在接下来的几年里，匿名者最引人注目的黑客攻击是为了支持2014年密苏里州弗格森的抗议活动。作为对迈克尔·布朗被警察枪杀事件的回应，该组织关闭了该市的网络服务器，并公布了警察局长的家庭地址。当官员们没有透露布朗死亡的细节时，匿名者泄露了紧急调度员讨论这一事件的录音。然而，当匿名者宣布枪手的名字时，它指错了人，损害了自己的声誉。
2006年，他发现4chan时只有12岁。当我为我的书采访他时，他回忆起Anons成群结队地进入聊天室攻击贝宝(PayPal)和万事达卡(Mastercard)的日子里的乐趣和“同志情谊”。但他十几岁的时候都在经济上苦苦挣扎，在零工经济的低薪工作之间跳跃。最终，他决定，作为一个“非自愿的独身”辍学生，他注定永远处于底层。他在2013年创立的4chan复制品8chan，成为“极右翼极端主义”广受欢迎的滋生地。然而，布伦南设法摆脱了他所说的圣歌的“有毒”意识形态；他的转折点出现在去年，当时一波自称为法西斯主义者的大规模枪击手都引用了8chan作为他们的灵感来源。从那时起，他一直在努力关闭8chan，现在被称为8kun。
另类右翼的萌芽一直是“匿名者”文化的一部分。虽然匿名巨魔军队在2007年开始骚扰新纳粹分子，但他们也在网站上涂上纳粹党徽和种族主义污蔑，以获取令人震惊的价值。最终，他们瞄准的新纳粹分子开始在他们的在线招聘活动中使用4chan。
因此，到了2016年，匿名黑客活动人士回到了他们曾经组织过的地方-4chan附近的聊天室和论坛-并开始发起后卫行动。2018年，“匿名者”向“QAnon”宣战，这是一种奇怪的另类右翼阴谋论，前一年由极右翼巨魔在4chan开始，但自那以来已蔓延到主流共和党话语中。
一些匿名黑客现在把时间花在追踪和揭露另类右翼组织者上，通常是在2000年代中期他们占据的同一个网络中。
有些议员改变了他们的工作方式。有几个人告诉我，他们现在工作得很安静，很少重蹈让他们中的许多人入狱的覆辙：宣传他们的所作所为。(然而，BlueLeaks并非如此。一名被确认为匿名者的黑客和其他匿名者组织很高兴在他们的旗帜下采用了黑客。)。
他们比以往任何时候都更加警惕，经常公开想知道他们中的谁是警察或线人。他们不再在古老的互联网中继聊天(IRC)上组织，认为它会被破坏，而是更喜欢更现代的端到端加密聊天客户端，如Wire、Gajim或Signal。对于社交媒体，他们几乎只使用Twitter，觉得其他公司在保护用户隐私方面做得不够。
年龄带来了节制。一位运营Twitter账号@Anon2World的匿名活动家告诉我：“自从这一切开始以来，我们已经成长了很多-至少我成长了很多。”“在2010-2012年间，我们会竭尽全力表明自己的观点；现在我们意识到，我们可能会不经意间以负面方式影响人们。”
许多成员强调，这一次，他们希望像2014年弗格森抗议活动期间那样，为“黑人生命也是命”(Black Lives Matter)发挥辅助作用，当时尽管他们跌跌撞撞，但他们的存在受到了一些BLM活动人士的赞赏。从长远来看，现在看来无名氏可能会永远与我们在一起，在革命性的时刻绽放，当它感觉到一次巨大的推动可能会带来改变的时候。
“匿名者”最初是从青少年在聊天室闲逛开始的。他们戴上反法西斯超级英雄的面具是为了好玩，但随着时间的推移，他们学会了首先用风格扮演这个角色，然后才是信念。
上个月，当青少年开始在不和谐的聊天室里闲逛，想知道如何加入匿名者时，来自最大的匿名者Twitter账户的答案很简单：自己动手。
许多新的匿名斯坦都来自TikTok和韩国流行音乐社区。5月底，韩国流行歌手用舞蹈视频堵塞了达拉斯警察局的举报应用程序。然后，在匿名推特账户的刺激下，他们预订了数十万张门票，参加特朗普在俄克拉何马州塔尔萨举行的命运多舛的集会，总统发现自己在那里发表了基本上是空座位的讲话。
这种模式感觉很熟悉：一群青少年在网上见面消费媒体，然后意识到他们的人数如此强大，他们可以搞一些史诗般的恶作剧，或者成为一个政治集体，或者两者兼而有之。正如前匿名会员杰克·戴维斯在推特上所说，“TikTok/KPOP…。感觉像是老4chan入侵/突袭…的更病毒式版本。我非常期待福克斯新闻台制作一些恐怖的视频，称他们是服用类固醇的黑客。“。
在“复仇者V”中，在2020年大流行导致法西斯独裁之后，每个人都戴上盖伊·福克斯的面具来推翻政权。
如果我们的世界和屏幕的另一边有什么不同的话，那就是。
